briefdescription.para: Transmitter or Receiver FIFO Full Handler.
Transmitter or Receiver FIFO Empty Handler.
These constants specify different types of handlers and is used to differentiate interrupt requests from the XSpdif peripheral.

sectiondef.header: AES Status and Register Masks and Shifts

description.para: For formats/line protocols check the AES Standard specifications document

briefdescription.para: Use of Channel Status Block bit shift.
Use of Channel Status Block mask.
Audio signal pre- emphasis bit shift.
Use of auxiliary sample bits bit shift.
Use of Auxiliary sample bits mask.
Indication of Alignment level bit shift.
Digital Reference Audio signal bit shift.
Sampling Frequency scaling flag bit shift.
Alphanumeric channel origin data register(s) offset.
Alphanumeric channel destination data bit shift.
Local sample address code register(s) offset.
Time-of-day sample address code register(s) offset.
Cyclic redundancy check character bit shift.

description.para: Register offsets for the XSpdif device.

briefdescription.para: Channel A user data bits 0 to 31.
Channel B user data bits 0 to 31.

sectiondef.header: Core Configuration Register masks and shifts

briefdescription.para: Is XSPDIF Core Enable bit shift.
Is XSPDIF Core Enable bit mask.
Is XSPDIF Reset FIFO bit shift.
Is XSPDIF Reset FIFO bit mask.
Is XSPDIF clock configuration bits shift.
Is XSPDIF clock configuration bits mask.
Transmitter or Receiver FIFO Full Interrupt bit shift.
Transmitter or Receiver FIFO Full Interrupt bit mask.
Transmitter or Receiver FIFO Empty Interrupt bit shift.
Transmitter or Receiver FIFO Empty Interrupt bit mask.
Start of Block Interrupt bit mask ( in Receive mode)
Transmitter or Receiver FIFO Full Interrupt bit shift.
This macro reads a value from a XSpdif register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is read from the register. The most significant data will be read as 0.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XSpdif core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file).

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value of the register.
C-style signature: u32 XSpdif_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: This macro writes a value to a XSpdif register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XSpdif core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file) to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

simplesect.para: None.
C-style signature: void XSpdif_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Callback function pointer type for SPDIF driver events.

detaileddescription.para: This typedef defines the function signature for callback functions used by the SPDIF driver to notify the application of asynchronous events.

parameterdescription.para: A void pointer to user-defined callback reference data. This parameter is typically used to pass context information or a pointer to the instance structure associated with the callback.

simplesect.para: The callback function should complete execution promptly to avoid blocking interrupt processing.
XSpdif_SetCallback

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the core. Initialization of the XSpdif includes setting up the instance data, and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif instance.
points to the configuration structure associated with the XSpdif.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

simplesect.para: None.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the XSpdif.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to soft reset the interrupt registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSPDIF instance.
is a pointer to the XSpdif instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the clock configuration bits.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Spdif instance.
is the clock division number. Clk_DivNum value can be only 4,8,16,24,32,48,or 64.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the Sampling Frequency (Fs) and returns it's value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif instance.
is the audio clock frequency value in Hz.

simplesect.para: - Returns the sampling frequency value in Hz.

briefdescription.para: This function reads all the Channel Status registers and writes to a buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif instance.
is a pointer to a buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the Channel A user data and writes to a buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSPDIF instance.
is a pointer to a buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the Channel B user data and writes to a buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSPDIF instance.
is a pointer to a buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a reference to an XSpdif_Config structure based on the core id, DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xspdif_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique core ID of the XSpdif core for the lookup operation.

simplesect.para: returns a reference to a config record in the configuration table corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.
None.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific Xspdif instance such that the driver is ready to use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif instance to be worked on.

is the unique id of the device controlled by this XSpdif instance. Passing in a device id associates the generic XSpdif instance to a specific device, as chosen by the caller or application developer.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the device was not found in the configuration such that initialization could not be accomplished.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the specified interrupt of the XSpdif.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif core instance.
is a bit mask of the interrupts to be cleared.

simplesect.para: xspdif_hw.h file for the available interrupt masks.

briefdescription.para: This function globally enables the final interrupt out to the system.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified interrupt of the XSpdif.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif instance.
is a bit mask of the interrupts to be disabled.

simplesect.para: None.
xspdif_hw.h file for the available interrupt masks.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified interrupt of the XSpdif.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif instance.
is a bit mask of the interrupts to be enabled.

simplesect.para: None.
xspdf_hw.h file for the available interrupt masks.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the XSpdif driver.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from the XSpdif peripheral, determines the source of the interrupts, clears the interrupts and calls call backs accordingly.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif instance.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function -------------------------------- ---------------------------------- (XSPDIF_HANDLER_START_OF_BLOCK) StartOfBlockHandler (XSPDIF_HANDLE_PREAMBLE_ERROR) PreambleErrHandler (XSPDIF_HANDLE_BMC_ERROR) BmcErrHandler (XSPDIF_HANDLER_TX_OR_RX_FIFO_EMPTY) TxOrRxFifoEmptyHandler (XSPDIF_HANDLER_TX_OR_RX_FIFO_FULL) TxOrRxFifoFullHandler

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSpdif core instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is a pointer to the callback function.
is a reference pointer passed on actual calling of the callback function.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: Performs a self-test on the SPDIF instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function executes a series of self-tests on the specified SPDIF (Sony/Philips Digital Interface Format) instance to verify that the hardware is functioning correctly.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSpdif instance to be tested. This parameter must not be NULL.

simplesect.para: Returns XST_SUCCESS if the self-test passes successfully. Returns an error code (XST_FAILURE or other error constants) if the self-test fails.
The instance must be initialized before calling this function. This function may temporarily affect the state of the SPDIF device during testing.
XSpdif_Initialize() for instance initialization.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the array i.e.

detaileddescription.para: Spdif_SrcBuffer which has the values of all the six Spdif AES status registers, extracts the required bits and displays them. Note: Call XSpdif_Rx_GetChStat API before calling XSpdif_Decode_ChStat API

parameterdescription.para: is an array that contains the values of all the the AES Status registers.It contains 24 bytes i.e. 192 status bits.

briefdescription.para: Soft Reset Register value to reset.
This file implements the channel status format related functions.For formats/line protocols check the AES Standard specifications document.

detaileddescription.para: Format status related offsets & masks definitions related to the channel status format.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release.

simplesect.para: None

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release.
